title: Select strategies for adding within 1000
description: Practice telling which strategies work for adding two numbers within 1000.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kanani wants to solve $663 + 94$.

So she adds $663 + 100 $, then subtracts $ 6$, to solve in her head. 

**Is Kanani's result equal to $663 + 94$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.
Yes, the results are equal because $663 + 94 = 663 + 100 - 6$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Denzel wants to solve $115 + 497$.

So he adds $115 + 500 $, then subtracts $ 3$, to solve in his head. 

**Is Denzel's result equal to $115 + 497$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.
Yes, the results are equal because $115 + 497 = 115 + 500 - 3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Keanu wants to solve $93 + 252$.

So they add $90 + 255$ to solve in their head. 

**Is Keanu's result equal to $93 + 252$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{93} + \goldE{252}&\stackrel?={90} + {255}\\\\
\blueE{93} + \goldE{252}&\stackrel?=\blueE{90} + \blueE{3} +\goldE{252}\\\\
\blueE{93} + \goldE{252}&\stackrel{\checkmark}=\blueE{93} + \goldE{252}
\end{align}$

We got exactly the same values on both sides of the equation.

Yes, the expressions are equal.
Yes, the results are equal because $93 + 252 = 90 + 255$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Puanani wants to solve $455 + 490$.

So she adds $445 + 500$ to solve in her head. 

**Is Puanani's result equal to $455 + 490$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{455} + \maroonD{490}&\stackrel?={445} + {500}\\\\
\blueE{455} + \maroonD{490}&\stackrel?=\blueE{445} + \blueE{10}+ \maroonD{490}\\\\
\blueE{455} + \maroonD{490}&\stackrel{\checkmark}=\blueE{455} + \maroonD{490}
\end{align}$

We got exactly the same values on both sides of the equation.

Yes, the expressions are equal.
Yes, the results are equal because $455 + 490 = 445 + 500$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Darius wants to solve $472 + 39$.

So he adds $470 + 30 + 2 + 9$ to solve in his head. 

**Is Darius's result equal to $472 + 39$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{472} + \maroonD{39}&\stackrel?=\blueE{470} + \maroonD{30} + \blueE{2} + \maroonD{9}\\\\
\blueE{472} + \maroonD{39}&\stackrel?=\blueE{470} + \blueE{2} + \maroonD{30} + \maroonD{9}\\\\
\blueE{472} + \maroonD{39}&\stackrel{\checkmark}=\blueE{472} + \maroonD{39}
\end{align}$

We got exactly the same values on both sides of the equation.

Yes, the expressions are equal.
Yes, the results are equal because $472 + 39 = 470 + 30 + 2 + 9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kapua wants to solve $158 + 565$.

So they add $150 + 550 +15 +8$ to solve in their head. 

**Is Kapua's result equal to $158 + 565$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{158} + \purpleD{565}&\stackrel?=\blueE{150} + \purpleD{550} +\purpleD{15} +\blueE{8}\\\\
\blueE{158} + \purpleD{565}&\stackrel?=\blueE{150} +\blueE{8} + \purpleD{550} +\purpleD{15}\\\\
\blueE{158} + \purpleD{565}&\stackrel{\checkmark}=\blueE{158} + \purpleD{565}
\end{align}$

We got exactly the same values on both sides of the equation.

Yes, the expressions are equal.
Yes, the results are equal because $158 + 565 = 150 + 550 +15 +8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leilana wants to solve $146 + 451$.

So they add $146 + 400 + 500 + 1$ to solve in their head. 

**Is Leilana's result equal to $146 + 451$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{146} + \goldE{451}&\stackrel?=\maroonD{146} + {400} + {500} + {1}\\\\
\maroonD{146} + \goldE{451}&\stackrel?=\maroonD{146} + \goldE{400} + {450} + \goldE{50} + \goldE{1}\\\\
\maroonD{146} + \goldE{451}&\stackrel?=\maroonD{146} + \goldE{400} + \goldE{50} + \goldE{1} + {450}\\\\
\maroonD{146} + \goldE{451}&\neq \maroonD{146} + \goldE{451}+{450}
\end{align}$

We added $450$ on the right side of the equation, but not on the left side.

So the left side of the equation is less.
No, the results are not equal because $146 + 451 < 146 + 400 + 500 + 1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kimimela wants to solve $905 + 37$.

So she adds $900 + 42$ to solve in her head. 

**Is Kimimela's result equal to $905 + 37$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{905} + \maroonD{37}&\stackrel?={900} + {42}\\\\
\blueE{905} + \maroonD{37}&\stackrel?=\blueE{900+5} + \maroonD{37}\\\\
\blueE{905} + \maroonD{37}&\stackrel{\checkmark}=\blueE{905} + \maroonD{37}
\end{align}$

We got exactly the same values on both sides of the equation.

Yes, the expressions are equal.
Yes, the results are equal because $905 + 37 = 900 + 42$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nereo wants to solve $49 + 262$.

So he adds $50 + 262 +1$ to solve in his head. 

**Is Nereo's result equal to $49 + 262$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{49} + \maroonD{262}&\stackrel?={50} + \maroonD{262} +{1}\\\\
\blueE{49} + \maroonD{262}&\stackrel?=\blueE{49} + {1}+\maroonD{262} +{1}\\\\
\blueE{49} + \maroonD{262}&\stackrel?=\blueE{49}+\maroonD{262} +{1} + {1}\\\\
\blueE{49} + \maroonD{262}&\neq \blueE{49}+\maroonD{262} +{2}
\end{align}$

We added $2$ on the right side of the equation, but not on the left side.

So the left side of the equation is less.
No, the results are not equal because $49 + 262 < 50 + 262 +1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Willka wants to solve $708 + 211$.

So he adds $700 + 203$ to solve in his head. 

**Is Willka's result equal to $708 + 211$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
{708} + {211}&\stackrel?=\blueE{700} + \maroonD{203}\\\\
\blueE{700} + {8} + \maroonD{203} + 8&\stackrel?=\blueE{700} + \maroonD{203}\\\\
\blueE{700} + \maroonD{203}  + {8} + {8}&\stackrel?=\blueE{700} + \maroonD{203}\\\\
\blueE{700} + \maroonD{203}  + {16} &\neq \blueE{700} + \maroonD{203}
\end{align}$

We added $16$ on the left side of the equation, but not on the right side.

So the left side of the equation is greater.
No, the results are not equal because $708 + 211 > 700 + 203$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Amarilis wants to solve $54 + 112$.

So she adds $52 + 110$ to solve in her head. 

**Is Amarilis's result equal to $54 + 112$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

If there are leftovers, they will show us which expression is greater.

$\begin{align}
{54} + {112}&\stackrel?=\blueE{52} + \purpleD{110}\\\\
\blueE{52} + {2} + \purpleD{110} + {2}&\stackrel?=\blueE{52} + \purpleD{110}\\\\
\blueE{52} + \purpleD{110} + {2} + {2}&\stackrel?=\blueE{52} + \purpleD{110}\\\\
\blueE{52} + \purpleD{110} + {4} &\neq \blueE{52} + \purpleD{110}
\end{align}$

We added $4$ on the left side of the equation, but not on the right side.

So the left side of the equation is greater.
No, the results are not equal because $54 + 112 > 52 + 110$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Denzel and Jada are trying to add $358+149$.

Denzel adds $358+150$ then subtracts $1$.

Jada adds $350+149$ then subtracts $8$.

**Whose result is equal to $358+149$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Denzel's strategy

Denzel adds $358+150$ then subtracts $1$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Denzel's expression}\\\\
\purpleE{358}+\blueE{149}&\stackrel?=\purpleE{358}+\blueE{150-1}\\\\
\purpleE{358}+\blueE{149}&\stackrel\checkmark=\purpleE{358}+\blueE{149}
\end{align}$

Yes, Denzel's result is *equal to* $358+149$.

###Jada's strategy

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Jada's expression}\\\\
{358}+\blueE{149}&\stackrel{?}{=}\maroonD{350}+\blueE{149}-\goldE{8}\\\\
{358}+\blueE{149}&\stackrel{?}{=}\maroonD{350}-\goldE{8}+\blueE{149}\\\\
\maroonD{350}+\purpleE{8}+\blueE{149}&\neq\maroonD{350}-\goldE{8}+\blueE{149}
\end{align}$

The original expression has an extra $8$, but Jada subtracts the $8$ instead.

So Jada's result is *less than* $358+149$.
Only Denzel's result is equal to $358+149$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Makaio and LaShawn are trying to add $171 + 665$.

Makaio adds $150+650+21+15$.

LaShawn adds $100+600+70+60+1+5$.

**Whose result is equal to $171 + 665$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Makaio's strategy

Makaio adds $150+650+21+15$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Makaio's expression}\\\\
\blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}&\stackrel?=\blueE{150}+\maroonD{650}+\blueE{21}+\maroonD{15}\\\\
\blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}&\stackrel?=\blueE{150}+\blueE{21}+\maroonD{650}+\maroonD{15}\\\\
\blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=}  \blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}
\end{align}$

Yes, Makaio's result is *equal to* $171 + 665$.

###LaShawn's strategy

LaShawn adds $100+600+70+60+1+5$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{LaShawn's expression}\\\\
\blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}&\stackrel?=\blueE{100}+\maroonD{600}+\blueE{70}+\maroonD{60}+\blueE{1}+\maroonD{5}\\\\
\blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}&\stackrel?=\blueE{100}+\blueE{70}+\blueE{1}+\maroonD{600}+\maroonD{60}+\maroonD{5}\\\\
\blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{171} + \maroonD{665}
\end{align}$

Yes, LaShawn's result is *equal to* $171 + 665$.
Both Makaio's and LaShawn's results are equal to $171 + 665$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Cameron and Aniyah are trying to add $341 + 293$.

Cameron adds $340 + 294$.

Aniyah adds $348 + 300$.

**Whose result is equal to $341 + 293$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Cameron's strategy

Cameron adds $340 + 294$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Cameron's expression}\\\\
\blueE{341} + \purpleD{293}&\stackrel?={340} + {294}\\\\
\blueE{341} + \purpleD{293}&\stackrel?=\blueE{340 + 1} +\purpleD{293}\\\\
\blueE{341} + \purpleD{293}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=}  \blueE{341} + \purpleD{293}
\end{align}$

Yes, Cameron's result is *equal to* $341 + 293$.

###Aniyah's strategy

Aniyah adds $348 + 300$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Aniyah's expression}\\\\
\blueE{341} + \maroonD{293}&\stackrel?={348} + {300}\\\\
\blueE{341} + \maroonD{293}&\stackrel?=\blueE{341} + {7} + \maroonD{293} + {7}\\\\
\blueE{341} + \maroonD{293}&\stackrel?=\blueE{341} + \maroonD{293} + {7} + {7}\\\\
\blueE{341} + \maroonD{293}&\neq \blueE{341} + \maroonD{293} + {14}
\end{align}$

Aniyah's expression has an extra $14$.

So, Aniyah's result is *greater than* $341 + 293$.
Only Cameron's result is equal to $341 + 293$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kalena and Raymundo are trying to add $855 + 52$.

Kalena adds $850 + 50 + 5 + 2$.

Raymundo adds $800 + 50 + 50 + 5 + 2$.

**Whose result is equal to $855 + 52$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Kalena's strategy

Kalena adds $850 + 50 + 5 + 2$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Kalena's expression}\\\\
\blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}&\stackrel?=\blueE{850} + \maroonD{50} + \blueE{5} + \maroonD{2}\\\\
\blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}&\stackrel?=\blueE{850} + \blueE{5} + \maroonD{50} + \maroonD{2}\\\\
\blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=}  \blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}
\end{align}$

Yes, Kalena's result is *equal to* $855 + 52$.

###Raymundo's strategy

Raymundo adds $800 + 50 + 50 + 5 + 2$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Raymundo's expression}\\\\
\blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}&\stackrel?=\blueE{800} + \blueE{50} + \maroonD{50} + \blueE{5} + \maroonD{2}\\\\
\blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}&\stackrel?=\blueE{800} + \blueE{50} + \blueE{5} + \maroonD{50} + \maroonD{2}\\\\
\blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{855} + \maroonD{52}
\end{align}$

Yes, Raymundo's result is *equal to* $855 + 52$.
Both Kalena's and Raymundo's results are equal to $855 + 52$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ashkii and Nuka are trying to add $487 + 396$.

Ashkii adds $500 + 400 $, then subtracts $ 13$.

Nuka adds $487 + 400 $, then subtracts $ 4$.

**Whose result is equal to $487 + 396$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Ashkii's strategy

Ashkii adds $500 + 400 $, then subtracts $ 13$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Ashkii's expression}\\\\
\blueE{487} + \maroonD{396}&\stackrel?={500} + {400} - {13}\\\\
\blueE{487} + \maroonD{396}&\stackrel?=\blueE{487} + {13} + \maroonD{396} + {4} - {13}\\\\
\blueE{487} + \maroonD{396}&\stackrel?=\blueE{487} + \maroonD{396} + {4}  + {13}  - {13}\\\\
\blueE{487} + \maroonD{396}&\neq  \blueE{487} + \maroonD{396} + {4}
\end{align}$

Ashkii's expression has an extra $4$.

So, Ashkii's result is *greater than* $487 + 396$.

###Nuka's strategy

Nuka adds $487 + 400 $, then subtracts $ 4$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Nuka's expression}\\\\
\blueE{487} + \maroonD{396}&\stackrel?=\blueE{487} + \maroonD{400 - 4}\\\\
\blueE{487} + \maroonD{396}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{487} + \maroonD{396}
\end{align}$

Yes, Nuka's result is *equal to* $487 + 396$.
Only Nuka's result is equal to $487 + 396$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eneida and Demairon are trying to add $107 + 139$.

Eneida adds $100 + 146$.

Demairon adds $106 + 140$.

**Whose result is equal to $107 + 139$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Eneida's strategy

Eneida adds $100 + 146$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Eneida's expression}\\\\
\blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}&\stackrel?={100} + {146}\\\\
\blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}&\stackrel?=\blueE{100 + 7} + \maroonD{139}\\\\
\blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=}  \blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}
\end{align}$

Yes, Eneida's result is *equal to* $107 + 139$.

###Demairon's strategy

Demairon adds $106 + 140$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Demairon's expression}\\\\
\blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}&\stackrel?={106} + {140}\\\\
\blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}&\stackrel?=\blueE{106 + 1} + \maroonD{139}\\\\
\blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{107} + \maroonD{139}
\end{align}$

Yes, Demairon's result is *equal to* $107 + 139$.
Both Eneida's and Demairon's results are equal to $107 + 139$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Odalis and Toya are trying to add $319 + 291$.

Odalis adds $320 + 290$.

Toya adds $310 + 300$.

**Whose result is equal to $319 + 291$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Odalis's strategy

Odalis adds $320 + 290$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Odalis's expression}\\\\
\blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}&\stackrel?={320} + {290}\\\\
\blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}&\stackrel?=\blueE{319} + \maroonD{1 + 290}\\\\
\blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}
\end{align}$

Yes, Odalis's result is *equal to* $319 + 291$.

###Toya's strategy

Toya adds $310 + 300$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Toya's expression}\\\\
\blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}&\stackrel?={310} + {300}\\\\
\blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}&\stackrel?=\blueE{310 + 9} + \maroonD{291}\\\\
\blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{319} + \maroonD{291}
\end{align}$

Yes, Toya's result is *equal to* $319 + 291$.
Both Odalis's and Toya's results are equal to $319 + 291$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Quillaq and Yamila are trying to add $178 + 193$.

Quillaq adds $180 + 195$.

Yamila adds $171 + 200$.

**Whose result is equal to $178 + 193$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Quillaq's strategy

Quillaq adds $180 + 195$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Quillaq's expression}\\\\
\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}&\stackrel?={180} + {195}\\\\
\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}&\stackrel?=\blueE{178} + {2} + \maroonD{193} + {2}\\\\
\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}&\stackrel?=\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193} + {2} + {2}\\\\
\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}&\neq  \blueE{178} + \maroonD{193} +4
\end{align}$

Quillaq's expression has an extra $4$.

So, Quillaq's result is *greater than* $178 + 193$.

###Yamila's strategy

Yamila adds $171 + 200$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Yamila's expression}\\\\
\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}&\stackrel?={171} + {200}\\\\
\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}&\stackrel?=\blueE{171 + 7} + \maroonD{193}\\\\
\blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{178} + \maroonD{193}
\end{align}$

Yes, Yamila's result is *equal to* $178 + 193$.
Only Yamila's result is equal to $178 + 193$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Elijah and Keone are trying to add $438 + 362$.

Elijah adds $440 + 360$.

Keone adds $450 + 374$.

**Whose result is equal to $438 + 362$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Elijah's strategy

Elijah adds $440 + 360$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Elijah's expression}\\\\
\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}&\stackrel?={440} + {360}\\\\
\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}&\stackrel?=\blueE{438} + \maroonD{2 + 360}\\\\
\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=}  \blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}
\end{align}$

Yes, Elijah's result is *equal to* $438 + 362$.

###Keone's strategy

Keone adds $450 + 374$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Keone's expression}\\\\
\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}&\stackrel?={450} + {374}\\\\
\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}&\stackrel?=\blueE{438} + {12} + \maroonD{362} + {12}\\\\
\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}&\stackrel?=\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362} + {12} + {12}\\\\
\blueE{438} + \maroonD{362}&\neq \blueE{438} + \maroonD{362} + {24}
\end{align}$

Keone's expression has an extra $24$.

So, Keone's result is *greater than* $438 + 362$.
Only Elijah's result is equal to $438 + 362$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Shanika and Kaipo are trying to add $549 + 366$.

Shanika adds $540 + 360 + 15$.

Kaipo adds $500 + 300 + 40 + 60 + 9 +6$.

**Whose result is equal to $549 + 366$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's break up or group the values to try to get the same addends in each expression. 

If there are no leftovers, the expressions are equal.

###Shanika's strategy

Shanika adds $540 + 360 + 15$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Shanika's expression}\\\\
\blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}&\stackrel?={540} + {360} + {15}\\\\
\blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}&\stackrel?=\blueE{540} + \maroonD{360} + \blueE{9} + \maroonD{6}\\\\
\blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}&\stackrel?=\blueE{540} + \blueE{9} + \maroonD{360} + \maroonD{6}\\\\
\blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}
\end{align}$

Yes, Shanika's result is *equal to* $549 + 366$.

###Kaipo's strategy

Kaipo adds $500 + 300 + 40 + 60 + 9 +6$.

$\begin{align}
\text{original expression}&\stackrel{?}{=}\text{Kaipo's expression}\\\\
\blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}&\stackrel?=\blueE{500} + \maroonD{300} + \blueE{40} + \maroonD{60} + \blueE{9} +\maroonD{6}\\\\
\blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}&\stackrel?=\blueE{500} + \blueE{40} + \blueE{9} + \maroonD{300} + \maroonD{60} +\maroonD{6}\\\\
\blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}&\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} \blueE{549} + \maroonD{366}
\end{align}$

Yes, Kaipo's result is *equal to* $549 + 366$.
Both Shanika's and Kaipo's results are equal to $549 + 366$.
